front: Installation: triangular table with 39 place settings, each dedicated to a famous woman from history, with hand-painted ceramic plates and embroidered runners
Installation: permanently housed at the Elizabeth A. Sackler Center for Feminist Art at the Brooklyn Museum
Installation: its plates use 'central core imagery' — vulva/butterfly shapes painted on each setting
Installation: its Heritage Floor is a porcelain tile base inscribed with the names of 999 significant people
Installation: Georgia O'Keeffe is the final place setting / guest of honor in this work
Installation: Alice Walker criticized the Sojourner Truth setting for depicting three faces rather than vaginal imagery — the only setting to do so
Installation: originally titled "Twenty-Five Women Who Were Eaten Alive"
Installation: includes settings for Artemisia Gentileschi, Virginia Woolf, Emily Dickinson, Sojourner Truth, and Sappho, among others
Installation: collaborative feminist work in which CalArts students refitted an abandoned Hollywood Victorian mansion with feminist artworks
Installation: co-organized with Miriam Schapiro; included a Nurturant Kitchen where eggs morph into breasts
Installation: included a bathroom overflowing with used tampons and a linen closet whose drawers bisect a female mannequin
Artist: companion piece to The Dinner Party — a quilt whose triangles represent different women from around the world
Artist: changed her last name from Cohen to Chicago and placed an ad in Artforum announcing the change
Artist: also created works titled Powerplay and Through the Flower
Artist: posed as a boxer in a 1970 Artforum photograph while wearing a shirt with her name
